approach: sweep remaining 16 aced behavioral-leaf specs one node at a time
re-derive scenarios purely from drawn CFG (no golden-set reference exists)

why: RATIFIED LIVE by the owner, scoped to ONE frozen scenario in define-agent.feature: the impl-producer entry scenario asserted the pre-doctrine behavior of co-producing a separate eval suite (one eval per frozen scenario). Current SKILL.md line 10 and the M1-ratified sibling define-skill hold the opposite — the frozen .feature IS the verification, so the impl-producer authors no separate eval file — so a conformant build FAILS the frozen scenario today. Rewritten (not deleted) to assert the current contract, preserving its pair with the standalone-mode scenario so the suite still asserts no eval file is written in either mode. No other frozen scenario touched; the other four repairs are additive and self-clear.

Re-derived define-agent's scenario set from the CFG drawn off SKILL.md's real control flow (29 edges, bound 1:1 in the rebuilt four-section README). Four unbound edges got additive scenarios: the workflow-skill defer to define-skill, the model-carried-when-named positive companion, the tools-present-for-delegated positive companion, and a gate-role naming scenario flagging a non-verdict action name (eval-runner) — a positive discriminator killing the denylist mutant the bare-verb scenario survives. One frozen scenario was stale (co-produces an eval suite the implementation stopped writing) and was corrected under the recorded owner Clearance above. Cold aced-spec-validator ALIGNED: additive scenarios well-formed and edge-distinct, README binds every edge 1:1, co-produces scenario independently verified STALE-CONTRADICTS from SKILL.md. Mechanical checks green post-commit; the align-spec narrowing flag is the cleared re-open.

Owner raised the leash live: self-assert the impl gate on a cold impl-judge PASS with clean scope and no frozen-scenario rewrite; STOP for owner ratification on any Clearance/re-open (frozen narrowing) or a CHANGE verdict. Spec gates self-asserted throughout.

RATIFIED LIVE by the owner (this node carried a Clearance, so it stops per the raised leash). Cold aced-impl-judge re-derived all five changed/added scenarios' oracles independently by static inspection of SKILL.md: workflow-skill defer, model-carried-when-named, tools-present-for-delegated, the eval-runner gate-role flag, and the corrected impl-producer-writes-no-eval-suite scenario all PASS. Adversarial check on the eval-runner scenario confirmed the broadened positive-form rule (Name rule + B9) genuinely flags a non-verdict action name, not just the bare-verb denylist. Scope clean: only two SKILL.md lines changed (the gate-role naming broadening); SKILL.md:10's impl-producer eval doctrine was already correct on main, so only the spec was stale. No collateral break in the other 24 scenarios.
